en: Fatigue, night sweats, weight loss preceded the heart attack. 18-year-old kid, suddenly an orphan, and a single father of two.
ko: 피로, 식은땀, 체중 감소가 심장마비보다 먼저 왔어. 18살, 갑자기 고아, 두 아이의 아버지.

en: Party of five. Powerful stuff. The OC of its day. Stress explains everything except the itchy feet.
ko: 파티 오브 파이브. 강렬한 소재야. 그 시대의 OC. 스트레스로 가려운 발 빼고 다 설명돼.

en: Athlete's foot covers that. Waiters work 12 hours a day in old sneakers. Good idea. Ignore the symptoms.
ko: 무좀이면 돼. 웨이터는 낡은 운동화로 12시간 일해. 좋은 생각이야. 증상을 무시해.

en: Makes her job easy. I'm not ignoring a symptom. I'm explaining a symptom. Kid's been hanging over a toilet despite anti-emetic therapy.
ko: 무시하는 게 아니라 설명하는 거야. 구토억제제에도 계속 토해.

en: Persistent vomiting could indicate increased intracranial pressure, tumor. Sure. Heart problems.
ko: 지속적 구토는 두개 내압 증가, 종양을 나타낼 수 있어. 물론. 심장 문제.

en: It's gotta be the brain. Thank God there's a neurologist in the room. Right. It's the feet. If only I were a podiatrist.
ko: 뇌일 거야. 신경과 의사가 있어서 다행이지. 맞아. 발이야. 족부과 의사였으면.

en: Probably caught an intestinal virus from one of those feral kids running around. Inflated vomiting causes an electrolyte imbalance, which leads to an arrhythmia, which leads to the heart attack.
ko: 야생 애들한테서 장 바이러스를 옮았을 거야. 구토로 전해질 불균형이 오고, 부정맥, 심장마비로 이어져.

en: And itchy feet. Nope. That's it? Nope.
ko: 가려운 발은? 아니. 그게 다야? 아니.

en: I've said too much already. This isn't a game, Miles. No, it's not. But it could be. What are you writing?
ko: 이미 너무 많이 말했어. 이건 게임이 아니야, 마일스. 아니, 게임은 아니지. 근데 될 수도 있어. 뭐 쓰는 거야?

en: Nothing. If you know the diagnosis, why don't— How are you gonna learn to swim? Unless I take off your floaties and throw you into shark-infested waters.
ko: 아무것도. 진단을 안다면 왜- 수영을 어떻게 배워? 튜브를 빼고 상어 바다에 던져야지.

en: You can't know what's wrong after a 30-second perusal of his file. Apparently you can't. Now, what's a game without rules?
ko: 30초 파일 훑어보고 뭐가 틀렸는지 알 수 없잖아. 넌 분명 모르지. 규칙 없는 게임이 뭐야?

en: No tagbacks. No biting. You get one test each, and the clock runs until lunch.
ko: 반칙 금지. 물기 금지. 검사 하나씩, 점심때까지야.

en: If I'm right, you'll still be alive. If I'm wrong, this is a very cruel game.
ko: 내가 맞으면 살아있을 거야. 틀리면 아주 잔인한 게임이지.

en: How are the feet? Are they still itch? Um, is that important? I don't know.
ko: 발은 어때? 아직 가려워? 중요한 거예요? 모르겠어.

en: Is it? What kinds of drugs are you into?
ko: 중요해? 무슨 약 해?

en: You don't have to talk about that right now. No, it's okay. I don't keep secrets from them. They know I did drugs before our parents died.
ko: 지금 그 얘기 안 해도 돼. 괜찮아. 비밀 안 가져. 부모님 돌아가시기 전에 약 한 거 알아.

en: Clever. Admit the past, deny the present. I'm clean. I'm raising two kids.
ko: 영리하네. 과거를 인정하고 현재를 부인하고. 깨끗해. 두 아이를 키우고 있어.

en: It'd be pretty irresponsible, wouldn't it? But confiding in an eight-year-old is okay. If I'm open with them, then they'll be open with me.
ko: 무책임하지 않겠어? 8살한테 고백하는 건 괜찮고? 솔직하면 애들도 솔직해져.

en: Yeah. Turning the other cheek's a good strategy in boxing. Kids don't lie because they got trust issues.
ko: 그래. 관용이 복싱에서 좋은 전략이지. 애들은 신뢰 문제로 거짓말하는 게 아니야.

en: They lie because they got something to hide. Look at him. Sitting there doing nothing. He's still lying.
ko: 숨길 게 있으니까 거짓말해. 저기 앉아서 아무것도 안 하잖아. 아직도 거짓말이야.

en: We don't lie to Jack. Spelling test you failed or forgot to? No. Math test? No. Fight with the kid sitting next to you?
ko: 잭한테 거짓말 안 해. 시험에서 떨어지거나 잊어버린 거? 아니. 수학 시험? 아니. 옆자리 애랑 싸웠어?

en: He doesn't sit next to me. The other one's probably having sex.
ko: 옆에 안 앉아. 큰 애는 아마 섹스하고 있을 거야.

en: I am not. Yet. But when you start, you're gonna lie about it. Does your arm hurt?
ko: 안 해. 아직. 근데 시작하면 거짓말할 거야. 팔 아파?

en: Arm's fine, bro. Then why is it bleeding? It's not. Place your arm above your head.
ko: 팔 괜찮아. 그럼 왜 피가 나? 안 나. 팔을 머리 위로 올려봐.

en: Your ear. And your nose.
ko: 귀. 그리고 코.

en: Now, osteomyelitis means you're wrong about foodborne toxins. And you can stop, too.
ko: 골수염이면 식중독이 아니라는 거야. 너도 그만해.

en: It's syphilis. You sure? Read the printout. He's also positive for eichenella.
ko: 매독이야. 확실해? 출력물 봐. 에이케넬라도 양성이야.

en: One of you two screwed up. No. Not a chance. This kid is a lot sicker than we thought.
ko: 둘 중 하나가 실수했어. 아니. 절대. 이 아이가 생각보다 훨씬 아파.

en: Finish that test. Should be impossible to give two right answers to one question. It's okay to have three?
ko: 검사 끝내. 하나의 질문에 두 개의 맞는 답이 나올 수 없잖아. 세 개는 괜찮아?

en: Apparently. He's positive for botulism, too. So we knock down one infection and three more pop up.
ko: 분명히. 보툴리즘도 양성이야. 하나 잡으면 셋이 튀어나와.

en: I think this game is rigged. Hey, Jack?
ko: 이 게임 조작된 것 같아. 잭?

en: Somebody help! Brainstem's clean. Nothing in the midbrain. What's that?
ko: 도와줘요! 뇌간 깨끗해. 중뇌에도 없어. 저게 뭐야?

en: Front of lower upper right quadrant. I don't see anything. There. It's tiny, but I think it's a tumor.
ko: 우상 전방 사분면. 안 보이는데. 저기. 작지만 종양인 것 같아.

en: That size should be excisable. Why didn't we see it earlier? Oh, God. Axial view.
ko: 저 크기면 절제 가능할 거야. 왜 전에 못 봤지? 세상에. 축 방향으로.

en: There's another one. Bring up the next slice. They're everywhere.
ko: 또 하나 있어. 다음 슬라이스 올려. 곳곳에 있어.

en: Little orphan Annie and Oliver Twist slept in our hallways last night. Are they sane? They're children.
ko: 고아 앤과 올리버 트위스트가 어젯밤 복도에서 잤어. 멀쩡해? 애들이야.

en: They need a guardian. I couldn't find a babysitter on account of not being able to make any phone calls, on account of not being able to breathe, on account of his lungs filling up with serratia.
ko: 보호자가 필요해. 베이비시터를 못 구했어. 전화를 못 했으니까. 숨을 못 쉬어서. 폐가 세라티아로 차서.

en: He has chronic granulomatous disease. Game over. Our work is done. Then he's gonna keep getting sick.
ko: 만성 육아종증이야. 게임 끝. 일 끝났어. 계속 아플 거야.

en: You gotta call social services. Bone marrow transplant would reboot his whole immune system. He'll be healthy enough to win miserable Daddy of the Year.
ko: 사회복지서비스에 연락해. 골수 이식으로 면역체계를 리부트할 수 있어. 비참한 올해의 아빠상 탈 만큼 건강해질 거야.

en: So see if one of his kids is a match. My kids are already testing his kids. You tested him? I never consented for that.
ko: 동생이 맞는지 봐. 이미 검사 중이야. 검사했어? 동의 안 했는데.

en: It's just a blood draw. There's no danger. To the testing? What about the surgery? The risk for Will is next to nothing.
ko: 채혈일 뿐이야. 위험 없어. 검사는? 수술은? 윌에게 위험은 거의 없어.

en: What if I don't do it? What are my other options? Your immune system can't fight off the germs we all come into contact with every day.
ko: 안 하면? 다른 선택은? 면역 체계가 매일 접하는 세균과 싸울 수 없어.

en: Now that we know, we can use more targeted medications. But you'll still get sick all the time.
ko: 이제 알았으니 더 정확한 약을 쓸 수 있어. 하지만 계속 아플 거야.

en: You'll be in and out of hospitals. Not all live. Jack, your lifespan will be substantially shortened.
ko: 병원 들락거리게 돼. 다 살지는 못해. 잭, 수명이 상당히 짧아질 거야.

en: And you'll be too sick to care for your brother and sister anymore. You shouldn't have pressured him.
ko: 동생을 돌볼 수 없을 만큼 아플 거야. 압박하면 안 됐어.

en: There's no pressure. He's eight years old. You tell him his brother's dying unless he helps, what do you think he's gonna do?
ko: 압박이 아니야. 8살이야. 형이 죽는다고 하면 뭘 하겠어?

en: He wants to help you. He has no idea. It's like you asked him to let me play with one of his toys.
ko: 도와주고 싶어 해. 모르는 거야. 장난감 빌려달라는 것 같은 거야.

en: This is surgery. He could die. He'll be fine. But you... You're gonna have a hard time protecting him like this if you're dead.
ko: 수술이야. 죽을 수도 있어. 괜찮을 거야. 근데 너... 죽으면 이런 식으로 보호 못 해.

en: I'll do it. When Will's 18, he can decide for himself.
ko: 할게요. 윌이 18살 되면 스스로 결정할 수 있어.

en: It's your lucky day. Just found another donor in the registry. Perfect match. We can do the transplant, no danger to the rugrat.
ko: 운 좋은 날이야. 레지스트리에서 기증자를 찾았어. 완벽한 일치야. 꼬마한테 위험 없어.

en: Just have to sign here. Unless there's another reason you don't want it.
ko: 여기 서명만 하면 돼. 다른 이유로 안 하려는 거 아니면.

en: There's a chance I'd die. A chance you'll be cured. Maybe you don't want that either.
ko: 죽을 수도 있잖아. 나을 수도 있어. 그것도 싫은 거 아니야?

en: Maybe hanging out in a hospital getting weighted on hand and foot reminds you of what life used to be like before you were forced to play Mr. Mom.
ko: 병원에서 편하게 대접받으면 아빠 노릇 하기 전 생활이 생각나는 거 아니야?

en: I don't like being sick. You don't want to be healthy either. This is your way out, guilt-free.
ko: 아픈 거 싫어. 건강한 것도 원치 않잖아. 이건 죄책감 없는 탈출구야.

en: I said I'd do it when Will's 18. He's able to take care of himself without you. Jack, your brother and sister need you.
ko: 윌이 18살 되면 한다고 했어. 그때 스스로 돌볼 수 있으니까. 잭, 동생이 너를 필요로 해.

en: I know! I know every second of every day that they need me, but I'm too young to be their dad.
ko: 알아! 매 초 매 순간 날 필요로 한다는 거 알아, 근데 아빠가 되기엔 너무 어려.

en: Good for you. Don't pretend you're surprised.
ko: 잘했어. 놀란 척 하지 마.

en: Thank you for not telling them. You're a good kid.
ko: 말 안 해줘서 고마워. 착한 아이야.

en: Three months from now, six months from now, you'll be visiting them and you won't be able to say goodbye.
ko: 3개월 후, 6개월 후, 면회 가서 인사를 못 할 거야.

en: You're gonna know you screwed up. They'll take his bone marrow and you'll take them back.
ko: 잘못한 걸 알게 될 거야. 골수를 가져가고 애들을 도로 데려올 거야.

en: And there'll be a burden and a pain and your life will never be what it was supposed to be.
ko: 짐이 되고 고통이 있고 인생이 예상과 다를 거야.

en: But you'll be proud of yourself. Your parents, they'll be proud of you.
ko: 하지만 자랑스러울 거야. 부모님도 자랑스러워할 거야.

en: I don't think so. It's what I want to believe.
ko: 그렇게 생각 안 해. 그렇게 믿고 싶어.
